video_title: The Best of #DDD Dishes Made by Guy's Friends 🔥 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Daybird
restaurants.guy_intro: We are in the home of a Tournament of Champions champ. From season four, the one and only champ, Chef Mei Lin.

dishes.description: Chicken thighs brined in buttermilk, double-fried, dipped in chili oil, served on a bun with hot honey, slaw, pickled shallots/chilies, and habanero ranch.
dishes.guy_response: Wow, that's so crunchy. One of the best chicken crunches I've had. It's very juicy. The full sandwich bite with the slaw, that's the kicker. Slaw is money. All the pickled veg in there is really dynamite. I love that little extra honey kiss, but definitely the habanero ranch is my go-to. Amazing ranch. It's a really dynamite sandwich.

restaurants.name: Black Market Liquor Bar
restaurants.guy_intro: Welcome back. We're at Black Market. Hanging out with the one and only Chef Antonia LoFaso.

dishes.description: True bolognese with pork, veal, beef, bacon, vegetables, white wine, milk, rosemary, served with handmade tagliatelle pasta, tomato sauce, and cheese stock.
dishes.guy_response: Antonio, this right here is in the top three best bolognese I've ever had in my life. And I've had the great ones. The rosemary kiss in this is pure genius. Bloomed in the butter, pulled at the right time. It's the ghost of flavor in the dish. The meat is broken down, the right amount of cheese. If you sold one thing in this restaurant and it was that dish, you'd still have a line out the door. That's a lights out dish.

restaurants.name: Little Goat Diner
restaurants.guy_intro: Now we're in the land of the Izard. You've seen me cook different foods from all over the world, so we take those flavors and turn them into diner classics, but our own twist on them.

dishes.dish_name: This Little Piggy (Szechuan Pork Sausage Biscuit Sandwich)
dishes.description: Szechuan cheddar biscuit with Szechuan pork sausage patty, hoisin mayonnaise, Szechuan maple sauce, and a fried egg, topped with strawberries.
dishes.guy_response: The biscuit's light, flaky, and tender and super flavorful. The Szechuan maple sauce is chuggable. The emulsified fat in the sausage patty is juicy, tons of flavor. And the strawberry. I would actually order more strawberries.

restaurants.name: Rusty's
restaurants.guy_intro: That's because owner Rusty Winkstern is used to working hard. After growing up on a farm in western New York, and he's been mixing farm fresh flavors with a taste of Texas since opening this joint in 95.

dishes.description: Layered casserole with chicken, cheese, tortillas, and a creamy sauce made with onions, bell pepper, celery, garlic, mushrooms, white wine, cumin, chili powder, Rotel tomatoes, chicken stock, and milk. Served with pinto beans and roasted root vegetables.
dishes.guy_response: So it's green chilies. They come through creamy, not spicy, which gets misinterpreted a lot of times when you talk about Tex-Mex food. Just like Matthew was saying, having that Dark Mate, I think you did the right balance. Yeah, real good consistency, man. Not too wet, not too dry. Shuffle down a plate full of that? Easily. That looks small. Cachalada. That's like Texas comfort food. Texas comfort food. Dude, nice job.

restaurants.name: Creek Street
restaurants.guy_intro: Creek Street is 180 from everything else we have in town. And since Matt, a.k.a. Randy, Watson, and John Corbo grew up here, they know what's around and exactly what's missing, even if they can't really explain it.

dishes.description: Pizza with sourdough crust, organic California tomato sauce, fresh mozzarella, ripped basil, pepperoni, garlic oil, finished with Sicilian oregano, pecorino, and hot honey (roasted habaneros, roasted garlic, honey).
dishes.guy_response: That's the one. The crust is outstanding. It's light. It's airy. It's got good sourdough, good yeast to it. This simple tomato sauce, where tomato is alive and well. A-plus pizza. A-plus, A-plus. It hits all the marks.

restaurants.name: Mabel's BBQ
restaurants.guy_intro: What is Cleveland barbecue? It's very inspired by Eastern Europe. Which is part of your heritage. Part of my heritage, but really part of Cleveland's heritage. So we have mustard barbecue, those Eastern European spices. All the wood is local fruit woods. So that's how we do all of our smoking.

dishes.description: Smoked pork belly with Cleveland-style barbecue sauce, pork rub, maple and mustard glaze, and coleslaw (green/red cabbage, cilantro, red onion, poppy seed dressing). Served with collard greens.
dishes.guy_response: The pork is cooked perfectly. I love this 300-degree pork. You actually got some texture out of the fat. Seasoned properly, the little maple mustard vinegar that came on the end of it was a nice little kiss. And the slaw, crunchy enough. Acidic enough. Super balanced. It's really great.

restaurants.name: Monster Ramen
restaurants.guy_intro: I put the winning money into this new restaurant I've been dreaming of. How did it become Monster Ramen? When it comes to ramen, I'm the monster.

dishes.description: Ramen with house-made noodles, clear chicken stock, beef chashu, beef jam, shoyu sauce, soft-boiled egg, bamboo shoots, wood ear mushroom, leeks, red onions, house pepper mix, sesame seeds, nori.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, that beef jam. You live 20 minutes from here, Moro? Yes, I do. I would be here every day. You're going to have my name at the bar on a brass flag. Dude. What are you doing here? This is a riot. Did you know he was here? This is Marcel Reese. This is one of my best friends in the world. I think he just drove by and saw the Camaro out there. Jeff, this is unbelievable. I've had a lot of dynamite ramen. This is Michelin star. Yeah. Wow. Astounding how much detail. But it tastes so warm and homey, and it doesn't taste pretentious. I'm in awe. This is one of the most exercised flexes in culinary that I've seen on Triple D. Delicious.

restaurants.name: Blue Owl
restaurants.guy_intro: That's because when Nadia Hajlooni took over this joint, she was looking to make everyone happy, including herself. Combining all of my interests, hospitality, cooking, business, music, bringing people together, this seemed like the perfect job.

dishes.description: Fried rice with shredded chicken (marinated in garlic, chili flakes, garlic salt, black pepper, turmeric), fried rice sauce, cabbage mix, green onions, cilantro, alfalfa sprouts, sambal chili paste.
dishes.guy_response: Talk about depth of flavor. It's all over the place in a very positive way. This is way bigger than fried rice. Love the fresh veggies. The shredded chicken thigh is my favorite. A good amount of cabbage to it. A little bit of heat on the side. And besides sharing this with Mark, I think it's awesome.

dishes.description: Burrito with marinated beef, fried rice, green onions, cilantro, spring mix, namtok sauce (fish sauce, soy sauce, lime juice, shallots, Thai basil, mint, lime leaves, sambal, chili flake, sugar, rice powder), and basil mayo in a toasted tortilla.
dishes.guy_response: Beautiful. I love the toasted tortilla. The fresh herbs inside of the namtok, also inside of this veggie mix with the baby greens, that herbaceousness. The fresh crunch and flavor of that sings in a different way than anything that's cooked. You get this tender, but seasoned beef. They're kind of like the little surprises. Those little nuggets. I've been to three restaurants today. I'm eating that burrito like it's the first thing I had all day. Mm. Oh. Damn it.

restaurants.name: Arizona Gold Wings
restaurants.guy_intro: Arizona Gold Wings in the window. Initially, we were like, oh, it's a new sports bar. Let's go watch the game. But the food is fantastic. And for David Lindsley, the restaurant biz has always been more than just a game.

dishes.description: Pizza with house-made dough, house-made barbecue sauce, shredded mozzarella, smoked pork, chicken, and brisket, house caramelized onions, finished with garlic butter, granulated garlic, oregano, white cheddar cheese curds, pecorino romano, and parmesan.
dishes.guy_response: In all honesty, when we started putting all those ingredients on the pizza, I thought it was going to weigh down that crust. But the way you cooked that pizza, the way the crust is house-made kept its texture, the structure, and the sauce works. That's a good balance. Really good pie. You're going after my job, aren't you? Marcel's masterpieces. Seriously. When you're a lot of things, you're trying to do barbecue, you're doing pizza, and so forth, there's going to be a flaw? I'm not finding it enough. Well done.

restaurants.name: Sweet Cheeks Q
restaurants.guy_intro: Chef Tiffany Faison is bringing legit Southern flavor up North. To New England. I've known Tiffany for a long time, and she's good at everything she does.

dishes.description: Chicken cured in a spice rub, then battered and double-dredged, deep-fried, and finished with a seasoning 'shake'.
dishes.guy_response: That's delicious. Really juicy. I mean, look at that. It's crying. Drippy chicken. Come on, bring the kids down for some drippy chicken. That's great, right?

dishes.description: Casserole with cream of mushroom soup, eggs, mayonnaise, diced onions, blanched broccoli, tons of cheddar cheese, black pepper, topped with crumbled crackers.
dishes.guy_response: No, it's delicious. What's delicious about it is the ratio of everything else to broccoli.

restaurants.name: Jose Enrique
restaurants.owner_chef: Carlos Rosario and Yaritza Rodriguez (Owners), Chef Gerardo Davila
restaurants.guy_intro: Cozy, delicious grandmother food, but it's fresher, it's brighter. We provide a good food that is the best way to feel like you're in Puerto Rico. And what better place for owners Carlos Rosario and Yaritza Rodriguez to show off their local culture than in a charming building facing an old San Juan plaza.

dishes.description: Sous vide chicken breast seasoned with olive oil, salt, paprika, oregano, garlic powder, stuffed with trifongo (boiled cassava, fried green plantain, ripe plantain, butter, garlic, salt), served with mamposteado rice and garlic cream sauce.
dishes.guy_response: I love the chicken. It's tender. It's not dry at all. And that garlic cream sauce definitely complements it. My favorite, though, is the trifongo. You get a little bit of garlic. You get a little kiss of sweet. Then you get the crunch. Then you get the cream. You haven't lost the texture of the rice with all of that liquid. I mean, delicious. Really good, Chef.

restaurants.name: The General Muir
restaurants.guy_intro: This is a great Jewish deli. Matzo ball soup. Everything's made from scratch. Got the pastrami sandwich. It feels like New York, with the tile to the ceiling. Biltwood chairs, patinaed brass, the whole bit, yeah. It's got the groove. And that groove comes from New Jersey transplant Todd Ginsberg, who got some advice about opening this joint with his partners in 2012.

dishes.description: Hash made with schmaltz (chicken fat), potatoes, diced pastrami (brined brisket, dry rub, smoked, steamed), salt, pepper, bell peppers, caramelized onions, cabbage, topped with eggs and parsley.
dishes.guy_response: Dude, the pastrami, it's the extra bark on the outside, that smoke. The added piece of the cabbage is totally key. Caramelized onions, really nice. Love the red bell pepper. You need that color in there. Nice and tender. It's unbelievable. What you guys are doing is absolutely setting the trend for a whole new style of deli.

restaurants.name: The Lanai
restaurants.guy_intro: The fresh ingredients. Like, really fresh. Because Jeremy Burwell's personal fish guy is Jeremy Burwell. I started selling fish to, like, little restaurants. And then decided to keep his catch for his own spot.

dishes.description: Poke bowl with sushi rice, fresh ahi, fresh ono, green onion, limu (seaweed), red onion, mango, watermelon, local avocados, fresh mint, basil, cilantro, macadamia nuts, ina mona (roasted kukui nut, red pepper), sesame oil sriracha mix, chili pepper shoyu.
dishes.guy_response: I've tried all kinds of flavors I've never had in a poke bowl. The mint, the watermelon. That watermelon is so good in there. That is so refreshing. Really nice. The kukui with the mint, game changer. You've got creamy, spicy, salty, nutty, crunchy. The macadamia nut, it makes the salad even better. It really does. It does. That's onalicious right there. That is delicious.

dishes.description: Poke bowl with sushi rice, half ahi, half ono, chili shoyu (sugar, soy sauce, Hawaiian chili peppers), green onion, sweet onion, red onion, limu, mango, local avocado, roasted kukui nut, furikake, roasted seaweed, sesame seeds, sea salt, sugar, chili oil.
dishes.guy_response: This has got a little sweet, got a little heat, some crunch. Mango avocado, of course, play well together. The chili pepper oil is next level. That gets it. I think I lost my taste buds. Good, now have the rest of it. I'll tell you how it tastes. I'll get back to you. No, hold on. Let me try it again. Yeah, fluke. There's no fluke in this. Delicious.

restaurants.name: The Silver Dollar
restaurants.owner_chef: Tyler Trotter and Lori Beck (Owners), Andy Myers (Chef)
restaurants.guy_intro: And that's the vibe Louisville natives Tyler Trotter and Lori Beck were going for when they opened this joint in an old house. Grill house was a home, so we wanted the food to reflect sort of the things that we grew up eating that we love.

dishes.description: House-made beef bologna, fried on a flat top with butter, served on a brioche bun with New York white cheddar, sunny side up egg, hot sauce, and baby mustard greens.
dishes.guy_response: Oh my god. That is straight up legit awesome. It's super flavorful. This is kind of a cross between a nice summer sausage and a bologna. The balance is really great. And the texture is the best part about it. This is dynamite. I like the egg. It makes it kind of saucy and messy. I don't like any sandwich that's not going to run down my arms. So especially in the morning, you know.

restaurants.name: Triple G's
restaurants.guy_intro: This used to be an old garage that worked on Volkswagens. That's why there's a Volkswagen on the roof. This old garage switched gears from cars to cooking in 2010, when chef and owner Greg Otten cruised over from a legendary local spot.

dishes.description: Burger with three five-ounce patties, three pieces of American cheese, house southern slaw, mustard, chopped onions, and house-made chili.
dishes.guy_response: Greg, I'm telling you, it's fantastic. You're attacking this thing. It's like a coyote got a hold of it. Yeah. Triple G burger. Triple G, sir. It's a sloppy piece of equipment, but it's delicious and filling.

dishes.description: Shrimp dipped in Dipsy Dog corn dog batter and deep-fried.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, yeah. They're so good. It tastes like a corn dog. Sweet, crunchy batter. Straight from the fair. Straight from the fair. Straight from the fair.

restaurants.name: Pigwich
restaurants.guy_intro: Pigwich is the restaurant portion. You can get sandwiches, burgers. It's all good Kansas City growth, man. We're sitting here. In farm country, and we're getting the freshest farm there is.

dishes.description: Sandwich with a pulled pork patty, fried pork tenderloin, three cheeses (pepper jack, cheddar, provolone), ranch dressing, andouille spread, house barbecue sauce, and B&B jalapenos on a bun.
dishes.guy_response: Wow. I love the crisp on the pork, and I'm loving the patty. Three cheeses, man, blend perfect. How about the jalapenos? Man alive. Bread and butter jalapenos is my new favorite thing. The ranch dressing, you need that cooling, creaming effect. My favorite part of the whole thing, though, is the pulled pork patty. My favorite part is eating it.
